title: ds-viz-bar - option matrix (light)
description: [LIGHT THEME] Test bench for splunk.bar (HORIZONTAL bars). IMPORTANT: bars are horizontal so the value axis is the x-axis and the category axis is the y-axis - the opposite of splunk.column. Each panel exercises a distinct option group: stack modes, barSpacing, seriesSpacing, dataValuesDisplay, dual-axis (xAxis2 in spirit, but uses overlayFields like the others), split sub-charts, and the sparkbar pattern. SPL is makeresults-based so this dashboard runs in any Splunk instance with no setup. Convention: hide y-axis title (the category name is already visible in labels), keep labels visible (auto), hide major tick marks.

options.query: | makeresults count=8 | streamstats count as i | eval category = case(i=1,"Auth",i=2,"Search",i=3,"API",i=4,"Web",i=5,"DB",i=6,"Cache",i=7,"Queue",1==1,"Storage"), requests = round(800 - (i*50) + random()%200) | table category requests

| makeresults count=6 | streamstats count as i | eval region = case(i=1,"US-East",i=2,"US-West",i=3,"EU-West",i=4,"EU-Central",i=5,"AP-South",1==1,"AP-East"), p50_ms = round(80 + 30*sin(i/2) + random()%15), p95_ms = round(180 + 80*sin(i/3+1) + random()%30), p99_ms = round(320 + 120*sin(i/2+2) + random()%50) | table region p50_ms p95_ms p99_ms

| makeresults count=6 | streamstats count as i | eval service = case(i=1,"checkout",i=2,"search",i=3,"profile",i=4,"auth",i=5,"catalog",1==1,"reviews"), errors_4xx = round(40 + 20*sin(i/2) + random()%10), errors_5xx = round(15 + 12*sin(i/3+1) + random()%6), timeouts = round(8 + 6*sin(i/2+2) + random()%4) | table service errors_4xx errors_5xx timeouts

| makeresults count=6 | streamstats count as i | eval service = case(i=1,"checkout",i=2,"search",i=3,"profile",i=4,"auth",i=5,"catalog",1==1,"reviews"), success = round(85 + 8*sin(i/2)), retried = round(10 + 4*sin(i/3+1)), failed = round(5 + 2*sin(i/2+2)) | table service success retried failed

| makeresults count=10 | streamstats count as i | eval product = case(i=1,"Headphones",i=2,"Laptops",i=3,"Phones",i=4,"Monitors",i=5,"Keyboards",i=6,"Cameras",i=7,"Speakers",i=8,"TVs",i=9,"Tablets",1==1,"Gaming"), revenue = round(1500 - i*100 + random()%200) | sort -revenue | table product revenue

| makeresults count=8 | streamstats count as i | eval product = case(i=1,"Phones",i=2,"Tablets",i=3,"Laptops",i=4,"TVs",i=5,"Audio",i=6,"Wearables",i=7,"Cameras",1==1,"Gaming"), delta_pct = round((random()%4000-2000)/100, 1) | table product delta_pct

| makeresults count=7 | streamstats count as i | eval region = case(i=1,"US-East",i=2,"US-West",i=3,"EU-West",i=4,"EU-Central",i=5,"AP-South",i=6,"AP-East",1==1,"SA-East"), users = round(50000 + 25000*sin(i/2) + random()%5000) | table region users
| makeresults count=15 | streamstats count as i | eval bucket = "Bin " . i, count = round(50 + 80*sin(i/2) + random()%30) | table bucket count

| makeresults count=6 | streamstats count as i | eval team = case(i=1,"Platform",i=2,"Mobile",i=3,"Web",i=4,"Data",i=5,"ML",1==1,"DevOps"), q1 = round(20 + 15*sin(i/2) + random()%5), q2 = round(28 + 12*sin(i/3+1) + random()%6) | table team q1 q2

ds_dualaxis.name: dual axis - revenue bar and conversion line overlay

options.query: | makeresults count=6 | streamstats count as i | eval region = case(i=1,"US-East",i=2,"US-West",i=3,"EU-West",i=4,"EU-Central",i=5,"AP-South",1==1,"AP-East"), revenue = round(50000 + 25000*sin(i/2) + random()%5000), conversion_pct = round(2 + 1.5*sin(i/3+1) + random()%1*0.1, 1) | table region revenue conversion_pct

ds_split.name: split series - small multiples by metric

options.query: | makeresults count=6 | streamstats count as i | eval team = case(i=1,"Platform",i=2,"Mobile",i=3,"Web",i=4,"Data",i=5,"ML",1==1,"DevOps"), incidents = round(2 + 5*sin(i/2) + random()%2), tickets = round(20 + 15*sin(i/3+1) + random()%5), deployments = round(50 + 30*sin(i/2+2) + random()%10) | table team incidents tickets deployments
| makeresults count=15 | streamstats count as i | eval bin = "b" . i, value = round(50 + 30*sin(i/2) + random()%10) | table bin value

options.markdown:
# `splunk.bar` - option matrix (light theme)

**Horizontal bars - the axes are flipped vs. splunk.column.** Categories on the y-axis, values on the x-axis. So `xAxis*` controls the value axis and `yAxis*` controls the category axis. **Convention:** `yAxisTitleVisibility:"hide"` (category name is in the labels), `yAxisLabelVisibility:"auto"`, `yAxisMajorTickVisibility:"hide"`. **Bar-specific:** `barSpacing` (between categories), `seriesSpacing` (within a category, between series), `stackMode` (auto/stacked/stacked100). Bar does NOT have `columnGrouping` (column-only). Per the 10.4 reference, `annotation*` options are listed as supported on bar but not in the option table - tested in column instead.

viz_basic.title: 1. Minimal - single series
viz_basic.description: Bare options applied. Categories on y-axis, value (requests) on x-axis.

viz_unstacked.title: 2. Multi-series side-by-side - latency p50/p95/p99
viz_unstacked.description: stackMode=auto (default). Three latency percentiles shown next to each other for each region. Width of each bar is the value.

viz_stacked.title: 3. Stacked - cumulative error breakdown per service
viz_stacked.description: stackMode=stacked. Total error count for each service is the bar width, broken into 4xx / 5xx / timeouts.

viz_stacked100.title: 4. Stacked 100% - request outcome share per service
viz_stacked100.description: stackMode=stacked100. Each bar normalises to 100%. Best for comparing PROPORTIONS across services rather than absolute counts.

viz_sorted.title: 5. Top-N sorted descending - revenue ranking
viz_sorted.description: Standard 'horizontal ranking' pattern. Sort happens in SPL (`| sort -revenue`); bar chart preserves row order. The longest bar at top reads naturally left-to-right.

viz_negative.title: 6. Negative values - delta with zero baseline
viz_negative.description: showXAxisWithZero=true forces the value axis (x in bar) to include 0 so positive and negative deltas share a common baseline.

viz_labels_all.title: 7. Data labels - all values shown
viz_labels_all.description: dataValuesDisplay=all puts the numeric value at the end of every bar. Often pairs with hidden value axis since the labels carry the precision.

viz_tight.title: 8. Tight bar spacing - histogram-like density
viz_tight.description: barSpacing=1 collapses the gap between rows so 15+ categories still read cleanly. Equivalent of a horizontal histogram.

viz_seriesspacing.title: 9. Visible series gap - paired comparison
viz_seriesspacing.description: seriesSpacing=4 creates a deliberate gap between series within each category - useful when you want q1 vs q2 to read as a clear pair, not as adjacent neighbors.

viz_dualaxis.title: 10. Dual value-axis - revenue bar + conversion line overlay
viz_dualaxis.description: overlayFields moves conversion_pct to the secondary axis and renders it as a line on top of the bars. Note: in bar charts the SECOND value axis is conceptually 'x2' but spelled `y2*` in options for symmetry with column.

viz_split.title: 11. Split series - independent x-ranges
viz_split.description: showSplitSeries=true breaks each metric into its own sub-chart. showIndependentYRanges=true (despite the name) gives each its own value scale - essential when comparing metrics with different magnitudes.

viz_hidden_chrome.title: 12. Hidden chrome - sparkbar (everything off)
viz_hidden_chrome.description: Sparkbar pattern: all chrome off. Use inside KPI tiles or executive cards where the bar chart accompanies a single value.
